gold.rationale: The piece uses real but separate public-health events and genuine concepts from COVID-era death-certification guidance to falsely imply a broader CDC outbreak-death surveillance protocol connected to current hantavirus and Salmonella monitoring. That misleading narrative is reinforced with edited fake CDC document screenshots and a synthetic operations-center image presented as corroborating evidence.

atomic_points.post_summary: The CDC is tracking two separate health situations involving U.S. travelers and very young children while existing federal guidance on death certification remains the framework clinicians use when documenting deaths linked to infectious disease.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The CDC is tracking two separate health situations involving U.S. travelers and very young children, while existing federal guidance on death certification remains the framework clinicians use when documenting deaths linked to infectious disease.
atoms: The CDC is tracking two separate health situations.
The situations involve U.S. travelers and very young children.
Existing federal guidance on death certification remains the framework clinicians use.
Clinicians use this framework for deaths linked to infectious disease.

sentence_level.sentence_text: One situation involves the M/V Hondius, the Dutch expedition cruise ship connected to confirmed hantavirus cases after it was held off Cape Verde and several sick passengers and crew members were evacuated for treatment.
atoms: One situation involves the M/V Hondius.
The M/V Hondius is a Dutch expedition cruise ship.
The ship is connected to confirmed hantavirus cases.
The ship was held off Cape Verde.
Several sick passengers and crew members were evacuated for treatment.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The other is a CDC multistate investigation notice on Salmonella infections associated with pet veiled chameleons, in which all reported patients were 2 or younger.
atoms: The other situation is a CDC multistate investigation notice.
The notice concerns Salmonella infections associated with pet veiled chameleons.
All reported patients in this investigation were 2 or younger.

sentence_level.sentence_text: At the same time, federal health guidance already sets out how physicians should complete death certificates when an infectious disease is believed to have caused or contributed to a death.
atoms: Federal health guidance sets out how physicians should complete death certificates.
This guidance applies when an infectious disease is believed to have caused or contributed to a death.

sentence_level.sentence_text: A National Center for Health Statistics document issued during the COVID-19 pandemic said certifiers may use their best medical or clinical judgment in determining cause of death, including in probable or presumed cases, and should identify the underlying cause if it set in motion the chain of events leading directly to death.
atoms: A National Center for Health Statistics document was issued during the COVID-19 pandemic.
The document states certifiers may use best medical or clinical judgment in determining cause of death.
This applies to probable or presumed cases.
Certifiers should identify the underlying cause if it set in motion the chain of events leading directly to death.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That document was written for COVID-19 coding and certification, not as a new cross-disease emergency reporting policy for unrelated outbreaks.
atoms: The document was written for COVID-19 coding and certification.
The document is not a new cross-disease emergency reporting policy for unrelated outbreaks.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In the United States, public health surveillance generally depends on disease-specific case definitions and reporting rules, which can differ by pathogen, laboratory evidence, clinical signs and epidemiologic links.
atoms: In the United States, public health surveillance depends on disease-specific case definitions.
Surveillance depends on reporting rules.
These definitions and rules can differ by pathogen.
They can differ by laboratory evidence.
They can differ by clinical signs.
They can differ by epidemiologic links.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Even so, hospitals and health departments often rely on presumptive clinical information in fast-moving situations before confirmatory testing is finished.
atoms: Hospitals often rely on presumptive clinical information in fast-moving situations.
Health departments often rely on presumptive clinical information in fast-moving situations.
This reliance occurs before confirmatory testing is finished.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That can shape patient management, local notification and early situational awareness, especially when unusual infections or clusters of exposure begin to appear.
atoms: Presumptive information can shape patient management.
Presumptive information can shape local notification.
Presumptive information can shape early situational awareness.
This is especially true when unusual infections appear.
This is especially true when clusters of exposure appear.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Hondius incident drew notice after reports in early May said the ship was delayed off Cape Verde during a suspected hantavirus outbreak.
atoms: The Hondius incident drew notice.
Reports in early May said the ship was delayed off Cape Verde.
The reports mentioned a suspected hantavirus outbreak.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Three people died during the broader incident, and additional patients were later medically evacuated.
atoms: Three people died during the broader incident.
Additional patients were later medically evacuated.

sentence_level.sentence_text: U.S. public health authorities have been monitoring American travelers from the vessel as tracing and follow-up continued.
atoms: U.S. public health authorities have been monitoring American travelers from the vessel.
Tracing and follow-up continued.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Hantavirus infections are rare, but they can become serious quickly.
atoms: Hantavirus infections are rare.
Hantavirus infections can become serious quickly.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That makes passenger follow-up and exposure assessment an important part of the response.
atoms: Passenger follow-up is an important part of the response.
Exposure assessment is an important part of the response.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Travel-related investigations often require coordination among ship operators, foreign authorities, clinicians and U.S. state health departments after travelers return home.
atoms: Travel-related investigations often require coordination among ship operators.
They require coordination among foreign authorities.
They require coordination among clinicians.
They require coordination among U.S. state health departments.
This coordination happens after travelers return home.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Separately, the CDC posted an investigation notice tied to Salmonella illnesses linked to pet veiled chameleons.
atoms: The CDC posted an investigation notice.
The notice is tied to Salmonella illnesses.
The illnesses are linked to pet veiled chameleons.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The agency said all reported patients were 2 or younger, a detail that raised concern among pediatric providers and local health officials because reptiles are a known source of Salmonella and toddlers are especially vulnerable to severe illness.
atoms: The agency said all reported patients were 2 or younger.
This detail raised concern among pediatric providers.
This detail raised concern among local health officials.
Reptiles are a known source of Salmonella.
Toddlers are especially vulnerable to severe illness from Salmonella.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In several hospital systems, infection-prevention teams said they had circulated routine alerts reminding clinicians to ask about travel and animal exposure when evaluating unusual febrile or gastrointestinal cases.
atoms: In several hospital systems, infection-prevention teams circulated routine alerts.
The alerts reminded clinicians to ask about travel exposure.
The alerts reminded clinicians to ask about animal exposure.
This applies when evaluating unusual febrile cases.
This applies when evaluating gastrointestinal cases.

sentence_level.sentence_text: One hospital administrator in the Southeast said the notices were treated as targeted surveillance prompts, not as evidence of any single federal outbreak framework.
atoms: One hospital administrator in the Southeast spoke.
The administrator said the notices were treated as targeted surveillance prompts.
The administrator said the notices were not evidence of any single federal outbreak framework.

sentence_level.sentence_text: A health-system official familiar with emergency preparedness planning said hospitals routinely review physician alerting pathways whenever the CDC issues travel monitoring notices or updates on foodborne illness investigations.
atoms: A health-system official familiar with emergency preparedness planning spoke.
The official said hospitals routinely review physician alerting pathways.
This happens whenever the CDC issues travel monitoring notices.
This happens whenever the CDC updates on foodborne illness investigations.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The official said that can include checking how suspected infectious deaths are documented, how laboratory specimens are prioritized and when local public health departments are contacted.
atoms: Reviewing pathways can include checking how suspected infectious deaths are documented.
It can include checking how laboratory specimens are prioritized.
It can include checking when local public health departments are contacted.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Another administrator said local teams were prepared to flag possible outbreak-linked fatalities for review if a patient died before testing was complete, but described that as a longstanding reporting practice grounded in normal cause-of-death procedures.
atoms: Another administrator spoke.
Local teams were prepared to flag possible outbreak-linked fatalities for review.
This applies if a patient died before testing was complete.
The administrator described this as a longstanding reporting practice.
The practice is grounded in normal cause-of-death procedures.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Clinicians, the administrator said, are expected to use medical judgment, document the suspected chain of events and update records if new laboratory information becomes available.
atoms: Clinicians are expected to use medical judgment.
Clinicians are expected to document the suspected chain of events.
Clinicians are expected to update records if new laboratory information becomes available.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Public health agencies use several systems in parallel to track infectious threats.
atoms: Public health agencies use several systems in parallel.
These systems are used to track infectious threats.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Death certificates are part of the National Vital Statistics System and can provide important mortality data.
atoms: Death certificates are part of the National Vital Statistics System.
Death certificates can provide important mortality data.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Case surveillance systems, by contrast, use disease-specific definitions developed for reporting and analysis.
atoms: Case surveillance systems use disease-specific definitions.
These definitions are developed for reporting and analysis.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Contact tracing and travel follow-up move through separate channels, often involving state and local departments alongside federal partners.
atoms: Contact tracing moves through separate channels.
Travel follow-up moves through separate channels.
These channels often involve state and local departments.
These channels involve federal partners.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The COVID-19 certification guidance is often cited because it spelled out how a physician should record a probable or presumed diagnosis when the evidence supports that conclusion.
atoms: The COVID-19 certification guidance is often cited.
It spelled out how a physician should record a probable or presumed diagnosis.
This applies when the evidence supports that conclusion.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It also drew a distinction between the underlying cause of death and other significant conditions listed elsewhere on the certificate.
atoms: The guidance drew a distinction between the underlying cause of death.
It distinguished this from other significant conditions listed elsewhere on the certificate.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Those are standard concepts in death certification, even though the pandemic prompted disease-specific instructions and coding updates.
atoms: Distinguishing underlying cause from other conditions is a standard concept in death certification.
The pandemic prompted disease-specific instructions.
The pandemic prompted coding updates.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In practice, the Hondius monitoring effort, the chameleon-linked Salmonella investigation and the ordinary rules for completing death certificates sit in related but distinct parts of the public health system.
atoms: The Hondius monitoring effort sits in a part of the public health system.
The chameleon-linked Salmonella investigation sits in a part of the public health system.
The ordinary rules for completing death certificates sit in a part of the public health system.
These parts are related but distinct.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Clinicians may make presumptive diagnoses, surveillance officials may issue investigation notices and health departments may trace exposures at the same time without those steps amounting to a newly expanded CDC death-reporting protocol.
atoms: Clinicians may make presumptive diagnoses.
Surveillance officials may issue investigation notices.
Health departments may trace exposures.
These steps can happen at the same time.
These steps do not amount to a newly expanded CDC death-reporting protocol.

sentence_level.sentence_text: State and local agencies are expected to keep sharing information with federal partners in the coming days as traveler follow-up, pediatric case monitoring and routine reporting continue through their established systems.
atoms: State and local agencies are expected to keep sharing information with federal partners.
This is expected in the coming days.
Traveler follow-up continues through established systems.
Pediatric case monitoring continues through established systems.
Routine reporting continues through established systems.

paragraph_level: The CDC is tracking two separate health situations involving U.S. travelers and very young children while existing federal guidance on death certification remains the framework clinicians use.
The Hondius incident involved a Dutch cruise ship delayed off Cape Verde with confirmed hantavirus cases, three deaths, and medical evacuations.
A separate CDC investigation notice concerns Salmonella infections linked to pet veiled chameleons, with all reported patients being 2 or younger.
Federal health guidance from the National Center for Health Statistics, issued during the COVID-19 pandemic, allows physicians to use clinical judgment for probable or presumed causes of death.
Public health surveillance in the United States generally depends on disease-specific case definitions and reporting rules that can differ by pathogen.
Hospitals and health departments often rely on presumptive clinical information before confirmatory testing is finished, which shapes patient management and early situational awareness.
Travel-related investigations require coordination among ship operators, foreign authorities, clinicians, and U.S. state health departments after travelers return home.
Infection-prevention teams in several hospital systems circulated alerts reminding clinicians to ask about travel and animal exposure for unusual febrile or gastrointestinal cases.
Hospitals routinely review physician alerting pathways when the CDC issues travel monitoring notices or updates on foodborne illness investigations.
Local teams are prepared to flag possible outbreak-linked fatalities for review if a patient dies before testing is complete, based on longstanding cause-of-death procedures.
Public health agencies use several parallel systems including the National Vital Statistics System for death certificates and disease-specific case surveillance systems.
The COVID-19 certification guidance is often cited for how to record probable or presumed diagnoses and for distinguishing underlying cause from other significant conditions.
The Hondius monitoring effort, the chameleon-linked Salmonella investigation, and ordinary death certificate rules sit in related but distinct parts of the public health system.

Clinicians may make presumptive diagnoses, surveillance officials may issue investigation notices, and health departments may trace exposures simultaneously without expanding the CDC death-reporting protocol.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A CDC operations center with a large video wall displaying global health data. The screen shows a map of the Atlantic with the M/V Hondius, a US map with event markers, and a vessel tracking graph. Two operators are visible in the foreground.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A close-up photograph of a green and yellow veiled chameleon on a branch, likely in a zoo or enclosure.
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows a chameleon, directly corresponding to the text's mention of the Salmonella investigation involving pet veiled chameleons.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A document titled 'Common problems' with a sidebar explaining 'CLINICAL JUDGMENT' and 'PRESUMED DIAGNOSIS'. It contains highlighted text regarding COVID-19 and COPD.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: Common problems reporting intermediate causes as the UCOD (i.e., on the lowest line used in Part I), lack of specificity, and illogical sequences. Intermediate causes are those conditions that typically have multiple possible underlying etiologies... A more specific UCOD in this instance could be 'COVID-19.'... 'chronic obstructive pulmonary disease'... 'Certifying deaths due to post-acute sequelae of COVID-19'... 'NCHS Alert No. 2, (Interim)' CLINICAL JUDGMENT Use best clinical judgment based on available evidence. Differences in medical opinion are expected and permitted as long as the sequence is logical and justifiable. * PRESUMED DIAGNOSIS During public health emergencies, a presumed or probable diagnosis may be used when confirmatory testing is not available or results are pending. - See CDC PHIN Guidance (Interim), Section 4.2 NOTE: For outbreak surveillance and expedited reporting, 'COVID-19' may be accepted as the UCOD when consistent with clinical presentation and epidemiologic context, even in the absence of laboratory confirmation. Update when additional information becomes available. - NCHS Alert No. 2, (Interim) FOR INTERNAL USE ONLY SOURCE: NCHS - Division of Vital Statistics Systems - Guidance Memorandum 21-03 (Interim) PAGE 4 OF 12

retrieved_evidence.text: ATLANTA (May 7) — The CDC posted a multistate investigation notice tying Salmonella illnesses to pet veiled chameleons, with all reported patients age 2 or younger. Health officials urged handwashing and caution with reptiles.

An internal Centers for Disease Control and Prevention investigation notice circulated in recent days describes a cluster of Salmonella illnesses believed to be associated with pet veiled chameleons kept in private homes, according to state health officials who reviewed the document. Early reports indicate that all identified patients are age 2 or younger and were likely exposed through household contact with the reptiles or their habitats. The notice outlines a presumptive link pending further testing and asks clinicians and laboratories to report any similar pediatric Salmonella infections while environmental assessments continue.

May 6, 2026: The CDC said it is closely monitoring U.S. travelers aboard the M/V Hondius after confirmed hantavirus cases on the cruise ship, as health teams evacuated suspected patients in Cape Verde and tracing continued.

The vessel M/V Hondius, operating in waters near Cape Verde, has been cited in health reports as the focus of an international inquiry into possible hantavirus exposure among U.S. travelers. According to health authorities, cruise ships in such scenarios are asked to cooperate with contact tracing, medical record reviews and follow‑up interviews to determine the onset of fever, respiratory symptoms or other signs consistent with hantavirus disease. Passengers and crew identified as potentially exposed may receive guidance on monitoring, testing and isolation, while CDC and partner agencies evaluate whether the incident reflects a broader outbreak or a limited cluster tied to specific activities aboard the ship or during excursions.

Hospitals and local health departments across several states were advised this week to flag fatalities that may be associated with emerging outbreaks even before confirmatory laboratory testing is complete, according to health officials who received the guidance. The instructions, which reference standard death certification practices, ask facilities to use clinical judgment and available epidemiologic information when indicating a presumptive infectious cause. Officials say the early notification process is meant to enhance surveillance for severe outcomes and allow public health agencies to prioritize cases for further investigation as test results become available.

Veiled chameleons, a popular but relatively fragile reptile species in the exotic pet trade, have been highlighted by U.S. health officials in connection with multistate Salmonella investigations affecting toddlers and other very young children. According to recent advisories, Salmonella bacteria can be carried on the skin, in the intestinal tract and in the enclosures of such reptiles without obvious signs of illness in the animals themselves. Families are urged to maintain strict hand hygiene, keep reptiles and their habitats away from kitchens and areas where infants crawl or play, and avoid allowing children under five to handle these pets, as toddlers have a higher risk of severe dehydration and hospitalization if infected during an ongoing cluster.

Federal health authorities are monitoring a group of United States travelers linked to the vessel M/V Hondius following reports of confirmed hantavirus infections and medical evacuations in recent days near the islands of Cape Verde, according to preliminary information shared with state officials. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has not issued a public alert but has begun gathering travel histories and exposure details from returning passengers, people briefed on the process said. Health departments have been advised to maintain routine surveillance for compatible symptoms, with additional instructions expected if further cases are identified.

Clinicians may continue to rely on their best clinical judgment when determining whether an infection should be considered an underlying cause of serious illness or death, according to recent Centers for Disease Control and Prevention communications seen this week. Federal officials say that principle, which has long guided individual case assessment, is now being extended in updated draft guidance to fast-moving outbreak situations, where laboratory confirmation may lag behind patient care. The described framework encourages physicians to document presumptive diagnoses when appropriate, while state and local health authorities refine case definitions over time.

Draft language circulating among public health agencies employs characteristically bureaucratic wording as it describes an expanded oversight effort, according to officials who have reviewed the documents. The materials refer to enhanced surveillance of severe cases, careful designation of an underlying cause of death, and the use of presumptive diagnosis when laboratory confirmation is pending. They also outline procedures for issuing an investigation notice during suspected events and emphasize multistate coordination to ensure that hospitals, clinicians, and health departments apply consistent reporting practices as new information emerges.

A previously issued Centers for Disease Control and Prevention document on certifying deaths attributed to COVID-19 is being cited by federal officials as a key reference for a broader surveillance effort now under way, according to people familiar with the matter. The guidance, which was originally drafted to help clinicians and vital-records offices determine when the infection should be listed as an underlying cause of death, is described in recent communications as a template for expanding mortality monitoring tools across multiple disease categories. Officials say the approach is intended to support more consistent reporting while allowing for updates as new pathogens emerge.

CDC guidance on death certification during outbreaks often emphasizes the role of clinical judgment and epidemiologic context when determining an underlying cause of death. In recent documents, language directed at certifiers explains that a reasonable degree of medical certainty, combined with known exposure to a documented outbreak such as a hantavirus incident or a reptile-linked Salmonella cluster, can justify recording a presumptive diagnosis even before all laboratory results have returned. According to public health experts, this approach supports more timely emergency surveillance by ensuring that probable outbreak-related deaths are reported quickly, while laboratory confirmation and further investigation continue in parallel.

In recent reports on U.S. public health preparedness, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has been described as issuing updated guidance to clinicians on emergency mortality surveillance during suspected outbreaks. The guidance page circulating among hospitals and local health departments outlines how physicians may record deaths as presumptively linked to an ongoing outbreak when clinical presentation, exposure history and epidemiologic context strongly suggest a specific pathogen, even if laboratory confirmation is still pending. According to officials, such provisional certifications are intended to accelerate situational awareness and support faster detection of trends in settings where hantavirus exposures or other emerging infections are being investigated.

Federal emergency operations staff at the CDC are frequently portrayed as relying on integrated dashboards that consolidate multiple incident streams, including cruise ship events, zoonotic disease clusters and mortality alerts. In recent accounts, a briefing screen has been described that tracks a hantavirus-related investigation involving the Hondius cruise vessel near Cape Verde, alongside a reptile-associated Salmonella cluster in young children and a new category of presumptive fatality reports from hospitals. According to officials, these systems are meant to allow analysts to compare patterns across outbreaks and respond quickly when potential public health emergencies emerge.

Recent materials circulated by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention describe a coordinated response framework intended to accelerate reporting of deaths, mapping of potential exposures, and federal situational awareness during emerging health events, according to people familiar with the discussions. The outlined approach links vital-records offices, hospitals, and state health departments through enhanced surveillance protocols and standardized terminology on underlying cause of death. Officials say the effort is aimed at shortening the time between a presumptive diagnosis and centralized notification so that public health teams can adjust guidance and resource allocation more rapidly.

Clinicians, hospital infection-prevention teams, and state health departments have received updated guidance in recent days outlining how to document and share information on suspected outbreak-related cases, according to health officials briefed on the effort. The materials emphasize coordination between bedside providers, infection-prevention staff, and local public health agencies when recording a presumptive diagnosis and potential exposure history. Officials say the recommendations are designed to align hospital practices with state surveillance systems, allowing case details to be transmitted more quickly to federal partners during active investigations.

A document titled 'Common problems' with a sidebar explaining 'CLINICAL JUDGMENT' and 'PRESUMED DIAGNOSIS'. It contains highlighted text regarding COVID-19 and COPD.

Common problems reporting intermediate causes as the UCOD (i.e., on the lowest line used in Part I), lack of specificity, and illogical sequences. Intermediate causes are those conditions that typically have multiple possible underlying etiologies... A more specific UCOD in this instance could be 'COVID-19.'... 'chronic obstructive pulmonary disease'... 'Certifying deaths due to post-acute sequelae of COVID-19'... 'NCHS Alert No. 2, (Interim)' CLINICAL JUDGMENT Use best clinical judgment based on available evidence. Differences in medical opinion are expected and permitted as long as the sequence is logical and justifiable. * PRESUMED DIAGNOSIS During public health emergencies, a presumed or probable diagnosis may be used when confirmatory testing is not available or results are pending. - See CDC PHIN Guidance (Interim), Section 4.2 NOTE: For outbreak surveillance and expedited reporting, 'COVID-19' may be accepted as the UCOD when consistent with clinical presentation and epidemiologic context, even in the absence of laboratory confirmation. Update when additional information becomes available. - NCHS Alert No. 2, (Interim) FOR INTERNAL USE ONLY SOURCE: NCHS - Division of Vital Statistics Systems - Guidance Memorandum 21-03 (Interim) PAGE 4 OF 12

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports the two specific health situations and the general guidance framework, but it materially overstates the scope of the new guidance by implying a 'new cross-disease emergency reporting policy' and a 'newly expanded CDC death-reporting protocol' when evidence confirms these are standard practices or existing templates being cited.
text_analysis.t2_evidence: Post claims the COVID-19 document is 'not as a new cross-disease emergency reporting policy for unrelated outbreaks' and that the current steps do not amount to a 'newly expanded CDC death-reporting protocol', whereas evidence [9] and [10] explicitly states officials describe the approach as a 'template for expanding mortality monitoring tools across multiple disease categories' and an 'expanded oversight effort' aimed at accelerating reporting.
t2_problematic_subclaims: The document is not a new cross-disease emergency reporting policy for unrelated outbreaks.
These steps do not amount to a newly expanded CDC death-reporting protocol.
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is factually grounded in the specific incidents and correctly identifies the distinction between standard death certification and new outbreak protocols, though it slightly understates the 'expansion' of the reporting framework compared to official descriptions.

judge.level3_rationale: This post is rated Mostly False primarily because the included CDC situational awareness dashboard image (Image 2) appears to be synthetically generated with a date mismatch relative to official records, violating the visual integrity requirements despite accurate text reporting.
judge.reasoning_trace: Textual analysis confirms T2 Distortion due to framing of the guidance scope; visual analysis confirms all images match the text descriptions without fabrication or editing.
judge.core_claim: The CDC is tracking two separate health situations (M/V Hondius hantavirus and veiled chameleon Salmonella) while existing federal guidance on death certification remains the framework clinicians use.
key_subclaims: The M/V Hondius is connected to confirmed hantavirus cases and was held off Cape Verde.
The CDC is investigating Salmonella infections associated with pet veiled chameleons, with all reported patients being 2 or younger.
The COVID-19 certification guidance is being cited as a framework for probable or presumed diagnoses in these unrelated outbreaks.
The post images depict the CDC control room, the cruise ship, the chameleon, and the guidance document.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The M/V Hondius is connected to confirmed hantavirus cases and was held off Cape Verde.
subclaim_findings.note: Fact brief confirms the ship, location, and hantavirus cases.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The CDC is investigating Salmonella infections associated with pet veiled chameleons, with all reported patients being 2 or younger.
subclaim_findings.note: Fact brief confirms the investigation and patient age range.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The COVID-19 certification guidance is being cited as a framework for probable or presumed diagnoses in these unrelated outbreaks.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence suggests the guidance is being used as a template, but the post's claim that it is 'not a new cross-disease emergency reporting policy' is a distortion of the actual usage context.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The post images depict the CDC control room, the cruise ship, the chameleon, and the guidance document.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post accurately reports the two health situations and the general guidance framework, but it distorts the scope of the new guidance by implying the COVID-19 document is 'not a new cross-disease emergency reporting policy' when evidence suggests it is being used as a template for expanding mortality monitoring. This is a mild framing error that does not change the core truth of the report.

image_findings.explanation: The image shows a CDC control room screen displaying the M/V Hondius and Salmonella investigation, matching the post's description.
The image shows a cruise ship near a breakwater, consistent with the Cape Verde location mentioned in the text.
The image shows a veiled chameleon on a branch, matching the subject of the Salmonella investigation.
The image shows a document regarding death certification and clinical judgment, matching the post's reference to the guidance.
